jd_text: Minimum qualifications: Bachelor's degree or equivalent practical experience. 10 years of enterprise sales experience in cloud, cybersecurity, or enterprise software, focused on agreements. Experience selling cybersecurity or cloud security solutions (e.g., identity, data security, threat detection, zero trust) to enterprise customers. Experience supporting customers in Qatar across public sector or regulated industries, including local compliance and procurement. Experience engaging senior technical and business stakeholders (e.g., CISO, CIO, CTO) to drive security transformation initiatives. Experience managing end-to-end sales cycles, including solution positioning, business case development, and contract negotiation. Preferred qualifications: Experience leading security-led digital transformation initiatives across cloud platforms, including AI/ML security, data protection, and modern architectures. Experience structuring and closing large, strategic, multi-year agreements with complex stakeholder environments. Experience partnering with GSIs, MSSPs, and ISVs to co-sell solutions. Understanding of cybersecurity trends, including zero trust, sovereign cloud, AI risk, and regional compliance. Ability to build and scale territories from inception, including pipeline creation, executive relationships, and expansion. Ability to communicate in Arabic fluently in this customer-facing sales role. About the job As a member of the Google Cloud team, you inspire leading companies, schools, and government agencies to work smarter with Google tools like Google Workspace, Search, and Chrome. You advocate for the innovative power of our products to make organizations more productive, collaborative, and mobile. Your guiding light is doing what’s right for the customer, you will meet customers exactly where they are at and provide them the best solutions for innovation. Using your passion for Google products, you help spread the magic of Google to organizations around the world. As a Security Sales Specialist supporting our security portfolio (User Protection Services, VirusTotal, Security Operations, Threat Intelligence, Security Command Center), you will drive growth of our cybersecurity business by developing strategic relationships with new and existing enterprise customers. In this role, you will partner with CISOs, security leaders, and executive stakeholders to define and execute security transformation strategies across cloud and hybrid environments. You will position Google Cloud’s security capabilities to address evolving threats, including identity-based attacks, data exfiltration, and risks associated with AI-driven workloads. You will lead complex, consultative sales cycles, articulating clear business value, demonstrating differentiated product capabilities, and aligning solutions to customer use cases and regulatory requirements. You will lead cross-functional teams across sales, customer engineering, product, and partners to deliver cohesive, outcome-driven engagements. You will build trusted advisor relationships, lead with a deep understanding of the customer’s threat landscape, and identify opportunities to expand adoption across security domains. By combining security expertise, commercial acumen, and executive engagement, you will scale impact across accounts and drive sustained value for customers and Google Cloud.Google Cloud accelerates every organization’s ability to digitally transform its business and industry. We deliver enterprise-grade solutions that leverage Google’s technology, and tools that help developers build more sustainably. Customers in more than 200 countries and territories turn to Google Cloud as their trusted partner to enable growth and solve their most critical business problems. Responsibilities Build relationships with customers as a subject matter expert and trusted advisor, managing business cycles, identifying solution use cases, and influencing long-term direction of accounts. Deliver against quota and achieve or exceed business and growth goals while forecasting and reporting your territory’s business. Work with Google accounts and cross-functional teams (e.g., Customer Engineering, Marketing, Customer Success, Product, Engineering, Channels) to develop go-to-market strategies, drive pipeline and business growth, close agreements, understand the customer, and provide excellent prospect and customer experience. Construct and execute an effective territory development plan. Work with multiple customers and opportunities simultaneously, understanding each customer’s technology footprint and strategy, growth plans, business drivers, performers, and how they can transform their business using our technologies. Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form .
